name: LinkAja Indonesian State-Owned Digital Wallet API
what_it_does: LinkAja Indonesian state-owned digital wallet and payment REST API for Indonesian merchants, government entities, and fintech platforms to accept LinkAja wallet payments, process QRIS QR code payments, and access LinkAja's government-backed digital payment infrastructure serving Indonesian consumers across Telkomsel's 170M+ subscriber base — enabling consumer wallet payment acceptance, QRIS interoperable QR payments, merchant settlement, and integration with Indonesian government service payment portals through LinkAja's national digital payment infrastructure. Enables AI agents to manage wallet payment for Indonesian state-affiliated consumer checkout automation, handle QRIS payment for interoperable QR payment automation, access payment status for transaction verification automation, retrieve settlement for merchant disbursement automation, manage refund for payment reversal automation, handle direct debit for recurring payment automation, access government payment for public service billing automation, retrieve loyalty for LinkAja point management automation, manage merchant onboarding for Indonesian business payment integration automation, and integrate LinkAja with Indonesian government portals, utilities, and state-affiliated enterprise platforms for national digital payment automation.
use_cases: Accepting LinkAja wallet payments from Indonesian consumers for merchant checkout automation agents
Processing QRIS QR code payments from all Indonesian e-wallet users for interoperable payment automation agents
Integrating LinkAja with Indonesian government service portals for public utility payment automation agents
Managing LinkAja merchant settlements and transaction reconciliation for Indonesian business finance automation agents
not_for: Non-Indonesian markets (LinkAja is Indonesia-specific state-owned digital wallet)
International cross-border payments (LinkAja is domestic Indonesian payment platform)
Non-Rupiah currency transactions (LinkAja operates in Indonesian Rupiah only)
best_when: An Indonesian merchant, government entity, or enterprise wanting AI agents to accept LinkAja wallet payments, process QRIS QR code payments from Indonesian consumers, and integrate with Indonesia's state-backed national digital payment infrastructure.
avoid_when: MERCHANT AGREEMENT REQUIRED: LinkAja merchant API access requires signed partner agreement; automated public API access assumption creates no merchant endpoint; automated must complete LinkAja merchant or partner onboarding. QRIS IS THE PRIMARY PATH: LinkAja implements Bank Indonesia's QRIS standard for interoperability; automated LinkAja-proprietary QR assumption misses cross-wallet acceptance; automated should use QRIS for broadest Indonesian consumer acceptance. OJK DIGITAL WALLET REGULATIONS: LinkAja operates under OJK (Otoritas Jasa Keuangan) e-money license; automated non-OJK-compliant implementation creates regulatory violation; automated must comply with OJK e-money and payment regulations. STATE-OWNED ENTERPRISE DYNAMICS: LinkAja is backed by Indonesian state-owned enterprises (Telkom, BRI, BNI, Mandiri, BTN, Pertamina, Jasa Marga); automated commercial-only API dynamics assumption misses government procurement and state enterprise integration requirements.

auth.notes: LinkAja uses API key + OAuth2 for merchant authentication. REST API with JSON. Jakarta, Indonesia HQ. Founded 2019 (merger of Telkomsel TCash, BRI Pay, BNI Yap!, Mandiri e-Cash, BTN Mobile, Pertamina MyPertamina). Products: Digital wallet, QRIS payment, direct debit, merchant payment, government services, loyalty. SDKs: None public. OJK licensed. Backed by Telkomsel, BRI, BNI, Mandiri, BTN, Pertamina, Jasa Marga. Competes with GoPay, OVO, DANA for Indonesian digital wallet.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox for approved partners; production requires partner agreement
pricing.paid_tiers: MDR per transaction; QRIS standard rates per Bank Indonesia regulation; government pricing negotiated
pricing.notes: Jakarta ID. OJK e-money license. QRIS standard MDR. State-owned enterprise backing. Government payment focus.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; payment APIs throttled per partner agreement

known_agent_gotchas: PARTNER AGREEMENT MANDATORY: LinkAja API requires approved partner/merchant agreement; automated open API access assumption creates no available endpoints; automated must complete LinkAja partner onboarding process before API development

USE QRIS FOR BROADEST ACCEPTANCE: LinkAja implements Bank Indonesia's QRIS standard; automated LinkAja-only QR assumption misses all other Indonesian e-wallet users; automated should generate QRIS codes for acceptance from GoPay, OVO, DANA, ShopeePay, and all QRIS-compliant apps

INDONESIAN RUPIAH ONLY: LinkAja processes IDR only; automated foreign currency assumption creates currency_not_supported; automated must handle IDR amounts with Indonesian locale (no cents, large denominations like Rp50,000)

OJK TRANSACTION LIMITS: OJK imposes transaction limits on e-money based on KYC verification level; automated unlimited transaction assumption creates limit_exceeded for unverified consumers; automated must handle OJK-mandated basic (Rp2M/month) and upgraded account limits

STATE ENTERPRISE PROCUREMENT COMPLEXITY: Integration with government entities and state-owned enterprises requires additional compliance documentation beyond standard merchant agreement; automated commercial-only onboarding assumption misses government procurement requirements
agent_readiness.security_notes: Indonesian state-owned digital wallet. OJK, Bank Indonesia QRIS. API key + OAuth2. Indonesia. Consumer wallet and payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Indonesian e-money. OJK, Bank Indonesia. API key + OAuth2. Indonesia. Consumer wallet and government payment data.
